title: specify statement "(A- => B) = 1" causes syntax error if "read_verilog -specify"
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```
read_verilog -specify <<EOT
module test;
specify
(A- => B) = 1 ;
(A+ => B) = 1 ;
(A => B) = (
 1.1, 2, 3,
 4, 5.5, 6.6 ,
 7.7, 8.8, 9,
 10.1, 11, 12
) ;
specparam b=1;
specparam [1:2] asasa=1;
endspecify

specify
specparam c=1:2:3;
endspecify
endmodule
EOT
```

(actually extracted from `tests/simple/specify.v`)

## Expected behavior

No error, similar to `read_verilog` (without `-specify).

## Actual behavior

`<<EOT:3: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected TOK_SPECIFY_OPER`
(followed by other errors on all statements after this)
